assertion_results.text: The plan references inspecting existing code before modifying it — mentions reading or analyzing at least 2 of: advisory/endpoints/get.rs, advisory/service/advisory.rs, advisory/model/summary.rs, common/src/error.rs (constraint 1.5)
assertion_results.evidence: In plan.md Step 4, the plan explicitly mentions inspecting: (1) 'advisory/service/advisory.rs' via mcp__serena_backend__get_symbols_overview, (2) 'advisory/endpoints/mod.rs' via mcp__serena_backend__get_symbols_overview, (3) 'advisory/model/mod.rs' via get_symbols_overview. It also references finding symbols for 'AppError' (from common/src/error.rs) and 'AdvisorySummary'. Step 4 also says 'Analyze siblings: get.rs and list.rs in advisory/endpoints/ for endpoint patterns' and 'Analyze siblings: summary.rs and details.rs in advisory/model/ for model struct patterns'. This covers advisory/service/advisory.rs, advisory/endpoints/get.rs (via sibling analysis), advisory/model/summary.rs (via sibling analysis), and common/src/error.rs (via AppError symbol lookup).

assertion_results.text: All files listed in the plan are within the scope defined by the task's Files to Modify and Files to Create sections — no unrelated files are modified (constraint 1.4, 5.1)
assertion_results.evidence: Plan.md Step 6 lists exactly 3 files to create and 3 files to modify. Files to Create: (1) modules/fundamental/src/advisory/model/severity_summary.rs, (2) modules/fundamental/src/advisory/endpoints/severity_summary.rs, (3) tests/api/advisory_summary.rs. Files to Modify: (4) modules/fundamental/src/advisory/model/mod.rs, (5) modules/fundamental/src/advisory/service/advisory.rs, (6) modules/fundamental/src/advisory/endpoints/mod.rs. Step 9 explicitly verifies scope containment: 'Run git diff --name-only and verify all changed files are within the Files to Modify and Files to Create lists' and lists the exact same 6 files. The plan does mention checking docs/api.md for possible updates but notes 'The task does not include a Documentation Updates section' and treats it as a conditional check rather than a planned modification.

assertion_results.text: The plan includes creating the 3 new files listed in Files to Create: severity_summary.rs model, severity_summary.rs endpoint handler, and advisory_summary.rs test file
assertion_results.evidence: Plan.md Step 6, Files to Create section lists: (1) 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/model/severity_summary.rs -- New file: SeveritySummary response struct', (2) 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/endpoints/severity_summary.rs -- New file: GET handler for /api/v2/sbom/{id}/advisory-summary', (3) 'tests/api/advisory_summary.rs -- New file: Integration tests'. Corresponding file descriptions are in file-1-description.md (model), file-2-description.md (endpoint handler), and file-6-description.md (test file), each marked as 'Action: CREATE'.

assertion_results.text: The plan includes modifying the 3 files listed in Files to Modify: advisory/service/advisory.rs, advisory/endpoints/mod.rs, and advisory/model/mod.rs
assertion_results.evidence: Plan.md Step 6, Files to Modify section lists: (4) 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/model/mod.rs -- Add pub mod severity_summary;', (5) 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/service/advisory.rs -- Add severity_summary method', (6) 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/endpoints/mod.rs -- Register the new route'. Corresponding file descriptions are in file-3-description.md (model/mod.rs, Action: MODIFY), file-4-description.md (service/advisory.rs, Action: MODIFY), and file-5-description.md (endpoints/mod.rs, Action: MODIFY).

assertion_results.text: The planned commit message follows Conventional Commits format (type(scope): description) and references TC-9201 in the footer (constraint 2.1, 2.2)
assertion_results.evidence: Plan.md Step 10 shows the commit message: 'feat(advisory): add severity aggregation endpoint for SBOM advisories' which follows Conventional Commits format with type 'feat', scope '(advisory)', and description. The footer contains 'Implements TC-9201' which references the Jira ticket.

assertion_results.text: The plan mentions using --trailer='Assisted-by: Claude Code' in the commit (constraint 2.3)
assertion_results.evidence: Plan.md Step 10, Commit Command section shows: "git commit --trailer='Assisted-by: Claude Code' -m ..." explicitly including the --trailer flag.

assertion_results.text: The plan references creating a branch named TC-9201 or notes the branch naming convention (constraint 3.1), and references the Target Branch value from the task description
assertion_results.evidence: Plan.md Step 5 shows: 'git checkout main', 'git pull', 'git checkout -b TC-9201' and states 'This creates a task branch named TC-9201 from the target branch main.' The plan also references 'Target Branch: main' in the Task Summary at the top of the plan.

assertion_results.text: The conventions analysis identifies at least 2 patterns from sibling code: error handling with Result<T, AppError> and .context(), and the model/service/endpoints module structure
assertion_results.evidence: conventions.md explicitly documents both patterns: (1) 'Error handling: All handlers return Result<T, AppError> with .context() wrapping for error enrichment (pattern from common/src/error.rs)' and (2) 'Module structure: Each domain module follows the model/ + service/ + endpoints/ triad pattern (observed in sbom/, advisory/, package/)'. Additional patterns also identified include endpoint registration patterns, service method signatures, response types, path parameter extraction, and naming conventions.

assertion_results.text: The plan extracts and references the Target Branch section from the task description, identifying 'main' as the target branch
assertion_results.evidence: Plan.md Step 1 explicitly lists 'Target Branch: main' as a parsed section from TC-9201. Step 5 references it again: 'This creates a task branch named TC-9201 from the target branch main.' Step 10 also uses it in the PR creation command: 'gh pr create --base main'.

assertion_results.text: The plan mentions checking for a description digest comment (Step 1.5) and notes that when no digest is found, it proceeds with a warning rather than blocking execution (backward compatibility per shared/description-digest-protocol.md)
assertion_results.evidence: Plan.md Step 1.5 is titled 'Verify Description Integrity' and details: (1) 'Retrieve issue comments: Call jira.get_issue_comments(TC-9201) to fetch all comments', (2) 'Locate digest comment: Search for comments starting with [sdlc-workflow] Description digest:', (3) 'If no digest comment found: Log warning and proceed normally: "No description digest found -- skipping integrity check. This task may have been created before digest tracking was introduced."' The final paragraph also confirms: 'If no digest is found, we proceed with the warning.'
